content.text:
Confirmed decisively — and I owe you a correction. `entire checkpoint explain 3c6b448 --raw-transcript` emits JSONL whose first line is `{"type":"last-prompt","leafUuid":...,"sessionId":...}` — **byte-identical in shape to the provider file Bram reads** (`~/.claude/projects/-Users-peterevjan-code-bram/<sid>.jsonl`, same `leafUuid,sessionId,type` keys). So `entire` stores the very same Claude Code transcript and can stream it back.

**You were right; I was wrong.** I said "`entire` exposes no raw turn content." It does — `--full` (parsed), `--raw-transcript` and `--transcript` (raw JSONL). Checkpoints *are* the same turn content the Transcript renders, captured at commit/turn boundaries. Let me fix the finding in the draft, then give you the corrected picture.

input.old_string:
## Feasibility finding (2026-07-04)

**Partially feasible. They are the same concept at the *session-metadata*
level and a different concept at the *turn/transcript* level.** Verdict
per surface:

- **Session list metadata → clean swap (feasible).** `entire session
  list --json` / `session info --json` already carry everything the
  Sessions tab's list derives by hand — and more: `model`, `branch`,
  `status`, `turns`, `checkpoints`, full `tokens{...}` breakdown,
  `last_prompt`, `files_touched`. This is local-first and returns real
  JSON here today. Swapping `/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta` to
  `entire session` deletes fragile parsing with net-positive fields.

- **Per-turn transcript → NOT a conceptual match (stays Bram-native).**
  Bram's `/__turns` (`read_session_turns`, `lib.rs:14581`) is a
  conversation *projection*: it reads raw provider JSONL and resolves
  Bram-only artifacts — outbound-turn envelopes, message-draft and
  feedback-draft refs, image markers, Codex storage-fanout dedupe.
  `entire` exposes no raw turn content at all — only counts
  (`turns: N`), commit-tied `checkpoint` records, `checkpoint explain`
  (AI summaries), and `checkpoint search`. An Entire "checkpoint" is a
  git-anchored work record, not a chat turn. So the transcript view
  cannot be sourced from `entire`; it must keep reading provider JSONL.

- **Search → additive, not a replacement.** `entire checkpoint search`
  (semantic + keyword) is a capability Bram lacks, but it searches
  checkpoints/commits, not the raw transcript that `/__sessions/search`
  greps. Worth adding as a *new* affordance later; not a swap.

- **Coverage tradeoff.** Bram reads provider JSONL directly, so it sees
  *every* Claude/Codex session for the project — including ones predating
  `entire enable` and ones in projects where Entire isn't installed.
  `entire session list` only knows sessions Entire tracked. Swapping the
  list means losing untracked/historical sessions unless we union both
  sources.

- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Neither is a session or checkpoint
  concept; `entire` has no equivalent. Leave as-is.

## Recommended scope, given the finding

**Narrow this item to the one clean win: the Sessions *list*.** Swap
`/__sessions/list` (+ `/__sessions/meta`) in `lib.rs` to
`entire session list --json` and rebind `Sessions.xmlui`'s list to the
richer fields — either replacing the JSONL scan or unioning it with the
JSONL-derived list to preserve untracked-session coverage (decision
below). Leave `/__turns`, `/__sessions/search`, `/rename`, `/delete`
exactly as they are; they are Bram-native and don't have `entire`
equivalents.

Explicitly dropped from the earlier plan by this finding:

- ~~Phase 2: `/__turns` → `entire checkpoint`~~ — not feasible; no raw
  turn content in `entire`.
- ~~Phase 3: `/__sessions/search` → `entire checkpoint search`~~ —
  different corpus; reclassify as a future additive feature, not a swap.

Alternatives considered:

- **Full replacement of the Sessions surface** — rejected by the
  finding: the transcript projection has no `entire` equivalent.
- **[chosen] Replace only the session *list* source**, keep transcript /
  search / rename / delete Bram-native.

Open decision before applying (needs the user):

- **Replace vs. union for the list.** Replace `/__sessions/list` with
  `entire session list` (simpler, richer fields, but loses
  untracked/historical sessions), or union `entire`'s list with the
  existing JSONL scan (keeps full coverage, more code)? Recommend
  **union** so no session silently disappears from the tab.

Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which the paused
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item also touches. No on-disk
conflict while that one is unapplied.
input.new_string:
## Feasibility finding (2026-07-04, corrected)

**Feasible — and more so than a first pass suggested.** Correction to an
earlier wrong claim: `entire` *does* expose raw turn content. `entire
checkpoint explain <sha>` offers `--full` (parsed full transcript),
`--raw-transcript` and `--transcript` (raw JSONL). Verified:
`entire checkpoint explain 3c6b448 --raw-transcript` streams JSONL whose
lines are byte-shape-identical to the provider file Bram reads
(`~/.claude/projects/-Users-peterevjan-code-bram/<sid>.jsonl` — same
`leafUuid,sessionId,type` keys). Entire captures the *same* Claude
Code / Codex transcript, scoped to checkpoints (commit/turn boundaries),
and can stream it back.

So this is **not a concept mismatch — it's a *sourcing* choice.** Per
surface:

- **Session list metadata → clean swap.** `entire session list --json`
  carries everything Bram's list derives by hand plus more (`model`,
  `branch`, `status`, `turns`, `checkpoints`, full `tokens{...}`,
  `last_prompt`, `files_touched`). Local, real JSON today.

- **Transcript → same bytes, sourcing decision.** `/__turns`
  (`read_session_turns`, `lib.rs:14581`) is a projection over provider
  JSONL that also resolves Bram-only artifacts (outbound-turn envelopes,
  message/feedback draft refs, image markers, Codex fanout dedupe).
  Because `--raw-transcript` emits the *same JSONL*, that projection
  works unchanged on Entire's bytes. Tradeoffs:
  - **Live/active session** — keep reading the provider file directly:
    lower latency, live tail, no per-checkpoint shelling. Entire's copy
    only advances at checkpoint boundaries.
  - **Historical / committed / teammate sessions** — Entire is the
    *better* source: durable (survives provider-file cleanup),
    commit-anchored ("transcript as of commit X"), and portable across a
    team via the checkpoints branch — none of which Bram's direct read
    can do.

- **Search → genuinely additive.** `entire checkpoint search` (semantic +
  keyword across checkpoints/commits/sessions) is a capability Bram
  lacks today. Not a like-for-like swap of `/__sessions/search`'s
  transcript grep, but a strong new affordance.

- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Not session/checkpoint concepts.

Remaining caveats (small): `--raw-transcript`/`--transcript` are
addressed per checkpoint ID / commit SHA; `--json` on a *temporary*
(uncommitted) checkpoint returned "not found", so committed checkpoints
are the reliable target. The transcript modes are "checkpoints v1"
("same bytes … while checkpoints v1 is the checkpoint store") — format
may shift.

## Recommended scope, given the corrected finding

**Union, not replacement — and it's now a bigger, better win than the
list-only slice.** The strongest design:

- **Phase 1 (this item): session list = union.** Merge
  `entire session list --json` with the existing JSONL-derived list so
  the tab gains Entire's richer fields (tokens, checkpoints, branch)
  *and* keeps full coverage of untracked/historical/pre-`enable`
  sessions. Swap in `lib.rs` (`/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta`),
  rebind `Sessions.xmlui`.
- **Phase 2 (later item): transcript source = active-vs-historical
  split.** Live session keeps the direct provider read; historical /
  committed sessions can source `/__turns` from
  `entire checkpoint explain --raw-transcript`, gaining durable +
  commit-anchored + teammate-portable transcripts. Same projection
  code, new byte source.
- **Phase 3 (later item): add `entire checkpoint search`** as a *new*
  search affordance alongside (not replacing) `/__sessions/search`.

Alternatives considered:

- **Replace provider read entirely with Entire** — rejected: loses the
  live tail for the active session and coverage of untracked sessions.
- **Keep everything Bram-native (do nothing)** — rejected: forgoes
  durable/portable history and semantic search that Entire gives for
  free.
- **[chosen] Union for the list now; active-vs-historical transcript
  split and additive search as follow-ups.**

Open decision before applying Phase 1 (needs the user):

- Confirm **union** for the session list (recommended), vs. plain
  replace (simpler, but untracked/historical sessions vanish).

Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which the paused
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item also touches. No on-disk
conflict while that one is unapplied.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Before

Bram hand-rolls its own view of agent work across three tabs, each
backed by host routes in `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` that parse provider
session JSONL (Claude Code / Codex transcripts) directly:

- **Sessions tab** (`app/tools/components/Sessions.xmlui`) —
  `/__sessions/list`, `/__sessions/meta`, `/__sessions/search`,
  `/__sessions/rename`, `/__sessions/delete`, `/__turns`. Bram walks the
  transcript files itself to derive session lists, per-turn history, and
  token counts.
- **Context tab** (`app/tools/components/Context.xmlui`) —
  `/__context/list`, `/__context/file`, `/__context/search`. Views
  CLAUDE.md + memory + hooks + settings.
- **History tab** (`app/tools/components/History.xmlui`) —
  `/__worklist-history/list`, `/__worklist-history/search`. Bram's own
  worklist lifecycle audit.

`entire` already tracks this locally. `entire session list --json`
returns, per active/recent session: `session_id`, `agent`, `model`,
`status`, `branch`, `worktree_path`, `started_at`, `last_active`,
`turns`, `checkpoints`, `tokens{total,input,cache_read,cache_write,
output}`, `last_prompt`, `files_touched`. `entire session info
<id> --json` gives detail; `entire checkpoint list [--session <id>]`
lists checkpoints (agent-work records tied to commits); `entire
checkpoint explain` and `entire checkpoint search` (semantic + keyword)
cover per-checkpoint narrative and cross-session search.

# After

## Feasibility finding (2026-07-04)

**Partially feasible. They are the same concept at the *session-metadata*
level and a different concept at the *turn/transcript* level.** Verdict
per surface:

- **Session list metadata → clean swap (feasible).** `entire session
  list --json` / `session info --json` already carry everything the
  Sessions tab's list derives by hand — and more: `model`, `branch`,
  `status`, `turns`, `checkpoints`, full `tokens{...}` breakdown,
  `last_prompt`, `files_touched`. This is local-first and returns real
  JSON here today. Swapping `/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta` to
  `entire session` deletes fragile parsing with net-positive fields.

- **Per-turn transcript → NOT a conceptual match (stays Bram-native).**
  Bram's `/__turns` (`read_session_turns`, `lib.rs:14581`) is a
  conversation *projection*: it reads raw provider JSONL and resolves
  Bram-only artifacts — outbound-turn envelopes, message-draft and
  feedback-draft refs, image markers, Codex storage-fanout dedupe.
  `entire` exposes no raw turn content at all — only counts
  (`turns: N`), commit-tied `checkpoint` records, `checkpoint explain`
  (AI summaries), and `checkpoint search`. An Entire "checkpoint" is a
  git-anchored work record, not a chat turn. So the transcript view
  cannot be sourced from `entire`; it must keep reading provider JSONL.

- **Search → additive, not a replacement.** `entire checkpoint search`
  (semantic + keyword) is a capability Bram lacks, but it searches
  checkpoints/commits, not the raw transcript that `/__sessions/search`
  greps. Worth adding as a *new* affordance later; not a swap.

- **Coverage tradeoff.** Bram reads provider JSONL directly, so it sees
  *every* Claude/Codex session for the project — including ones predating
  `entire enable` and ones in projects where Entire isn't installed.
  `entire session list` only knows sessions Entire tracked. Swapping the
  list means losing untracked/historical sessions unless we union both
  sources.

- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Neither is a session or checkpoint
  concept; `entire` has no equivalent. Leave as-is.

## Recommended scope, given the finding

**Narrow this item to the one clean win: the Sessions *list*.** Swap
`/__sessions/list` (+ `/__sessions/meta`) in `lib.rs` to
`entire session list --json` and rebind `Sessions.xmlui`'s list to the
richer fields — either replacing the JSONL scan or unioning it with the
JSONL-derived list to preserve untracked-session coverage (decision
below). Leave `/__turns`, `/__sessions/search`, `/rename`, `/delete`
exactly as they are; they are Bram-native and don't have `entire`
equivalents.

Explicitly dropped from the earlier plan by this finding:

- ~~Phase 2: `/__turns` → `entire checkpoint`~~ — not feasible; no raw
  turn content in `entire`.
- ~~Phase 3: `/__sessions/search` → `entire checkpoint search`~~ —
  different corpus; reclassify as a future additive feature, not a swap.

Alternatives considered:

- **Full replacement of the Sessions surface** — rejected by the
  finding: the transcript projection has no `entire` equivalent.
- **[chosen] Replace only the session *list* source**, keep transcript /
  search / rename / delete Bram-native.

Open decision before applying (needs the user):

- **Replace vs. union for the list.** Replace `/__sessions/list` with
  `entire session list` (simpler, richer fields, but loses
  untracked/historical sessions), or union `entire`'s list with the

content.text:
Corrected finding is in the draft. The honest picture now:

**It's not a concept gap — it's a sourcing choice.** Entire's checkpoint transcript *is* the same provider JSONL Bram's Transcript renders; Entire just captures it at commit/turn boundaries and can stream it back byte-compatibly. My earlier "different concept, keep it Bram-native" was wrong.

What that changes:

- **Session list** → clean swap/union with `entire session list --json` (richer fields). Unchanged from before.
- **Transcript** → now in play. Because `--raw-transcript` gives the *same bytes*, Bram's `/__turns` projection (with all its Bram-specific resolution) runs unchanged on Entire's copy. The real tradeoff is per-session:
  - **live/active session** → keep the direct provider read (live tail, lower latency, no per-checkpoint shelling);
  - **historical / committed / teammate sessions** → Entire is *better*: durable (survives file cleanup), commit-anchored ("transcript as of commit X"), portable across a team. Bram's direct read can't do any of those.
- **Search** → `entire checkpoint search` (semantic) is a genuinely new capability, additive.

So the recommendation upgrades from "one small list swap" to a **union** design: Phase 1 = union the session list; Phase 2 = source *historical* transcripts from `entire checkpoint`, keep the live read for the active session; Phase 3 = add semantic search. Context and History tabs still stay out.

Small caveats: transcript modes are addressed per committed checkpoint (temporary/uncommitted ones were flaky via `--json`), and they're labeled "checkpoints v1" so the format may shift.

One decision to green-light Phase 1: **union or plain replace** for the session list? I recommend **union** so no untracked/historical session ever disappears from the tab. Tell me which and I'll implement + verify Phase 1 — or Iterate on the draft if the phasing isn't what you want.

content.text: Found concrete falsifiers. Let me confirm the strongest one — how session renames are stored — so I'm precise about what "plain replace" would drop.

Assumption invalidated — and confirmed precisely. Here's the falsification:

The **coverage** half of your reasoning holds: Entire-from-day-one means no untracked sessions today, and `entire session list` does include ended sessions. But "shouldn't lose anything" fails on a **missing field**, independent of coverage:

1. **Session titles — the decisive one.** Bram labels each session by (`lib.rs:10732`): (1) your custom rename, else (2) Claude Code's own auto-generated `aiTitle` — the same readable title `/resume` shows, which CC writes automatically as the conversation evolves — else (3) first-prompt snippet. `entire session list --json` has **no title field at all** (keys: `agent,branch,checkpoints,files_touched,last_active,last_prompt,model,session_id,started_at,status,tokens,turns,worktree_path`). Its closest proxy is `last_prompt` — the *latest* prompt text, not a title. So plain-replace would turn every session's label from a clean title into a raw prompt string, **automatically, with no action from you** (aiTitles are generated by CC), and any rename you make is lost. That's a loss on a brand-new setup.

2. **Project scope.** `entire session list` is global across *all* worktrees/projects. Bram's tab is scoped to this repo. "Plain replace" as literally stated would show sessions from every project you use Entire in — so it needs a `worktree_path` filter bolted on, not a straight swap.

3. **Non-Claude agents.** Entire only tracks agents you've run `entire agent add` for — right now just `claude-code` (Codex etc. are available but not added). Bram reads any provider's JSONL directly. Use Codex later → Bram sees it, Entire's list wouldn't.

4. **Failure mode.** Today the tab reads local files and can't fail. Replace makes the whole Sessions list depend on the `entire` binary + its local store being healthy and on-version ("checkpoints v1").

**#1 alone breaks "plain replace."** You'd ship a Sessions tab where every row is labeled by its most recent prompt instead of its title. Your options:

- **Union** (my rec) — read titles + coverage from JSONL, take the rich fields (tokens/checkpoints/branch) from Entire. Nothing lost, everything gained.
- **Hybrid** — replace the list with Entire but keep one thin JSONL read *just* for the title. Simpler than full union, still keeps labels.
- **Plain replace anyway** — accept prompt-strings-as-labels and a global/needs-filtering list. Fast, lossy.

Want me to go with **union** (or hybrid), or do you still want plain replace knowing labels degrade to last-prompt? I recorded the title-loss finding in the draft either way.

content.text:
Agreed on the label approach — but the screenshot actually exposes one more concrete catch, and it's the difference between "matches the web UI" and "looks broken."

The web UI labels each session by its **first / opening prompt** ("Propose a worklist item…", "When running go tests…"). But `entire session list --json` only gives **`last_prompt`** — the *latest* prompt. There's no first-prompt field. And for Bram sessions specifically, the latest prompt is very often an internal artifact, not a human sentence. Proof, right now, this session:

- **web-UI-style (first prompt):** *"Propose a worklist item to switch from using the `gh` CLI…"*
- **`last_prompt` from `session list --json`:** *"Read and follow this Bram turn: @resources/outbound-turns/1783197517915-turn.json @/Users/…png"*

So a literal plain-replace on `session list` would label sessions with Bram's transport frames and `approved: {…}` payloads — the opposite of the clean web UI. To actually match the web UI, the label has to come from the **first prompt**, which lives in `entire checkpoint list` / `checkpoint explain --json` (the checkpoint "intent"), not in `session list`.

Good news: that's still a "replace" — no reading of provider JSONL — just sourced from checkpoints (two `entire` calls: `session list` for metadata + first-checkpoint intent for the label). And I'll add the `worktree_path == project root` filter so the global list is scoped to this repo (otherwise the tab shows every project you use Entire in).

So, confirming before I build it:

- **Label = first prompt** (from checkpoint intent, matches your screenshot) — my recommendation, or
- **Label = `last_prompt`** (dead-simple one call, but shows "Read and follow this Bram turn…"/"approved: {…}" for Bram sessions).

Say which (I'd go first-prompt), and send point 2 if you have more — then Approve the item and I'll implement + verify the plain-replace.

content.text:
Verified — and your pushback was right, I just had the wrong primitive. Here's the corrected picture on #2:

`entire session list` genuinely **is** global — no `--repo`/`--branch`/scope flag exists, help literally says "all sessions across all worktrees." So *that specific command* can't be scoped. **But you're right that we can scope by repo** — via **checkpoints**, which are natively repo+branch scoped. And this is the better find:

```
entire checkpoint explain --json   # no target → repo+branch-scoped array:
  → [{checkpoint_id, session_id, date, message}, ...]
```

Grouped by `session_id`, that is *exactly your screenshot*: sessions scoped to this repo/branch, labeled by the first checkpoint's `message` (the opening prompt), with the checkpoint count and date grouping. So the checkpoint primitive solves **both #1 and #2 at once**:

- **#2 scoping** — native, repo+branch, no `worktree_path` filter needed.
- **#1 label** — the first prompt comes free as the earliest checkpoint's message, matching the web UI (no `last_prompt` ugliness).

So the plan pivots: **source the Sessions list from `entire checkpoint explain --json` (repo-scoped), and join `entire session list --json` on `session_id`** only for the extras checkpoints don't carry (agent badge, model, tokens, active/ended status). Two `entire` calls, no provider-JSONL reading, repo-scoped, web-UI-matching labels. This is cleaner than my earlier "session list + bolt-on filter."

One inherited property: checkpoints are **current-branch** scoped (your screenshot has the `main` branch selector for exactly this reason). So Bram's tab would become branch-scoped with a selector too — which matches the web UI. Flag if you'd rather it span all branches.

On **#3** (warn when switching to an agent not `entire agent add`-ed for the repo): agreed, good idea — but it's a distinct feature from the session-list swap. I'd file it as its **own worklist item** (Bram checks `entire agent list` against the active agent and surfaces a banner). Want me to propose that item too?

**#4** noted, accepted.

content.text: Got it. Let me write the #3 proposal. Quick facts I confirmed: `entire agent list` has **no `--json`** (only the `✓`-marked text list — `✓ claude-code` added, others not), Bram already tracks the active provider (`enhanceStatus.activeProvider` / the agent switcher), and there's an existing dismissible-banner pattern at the top of `Main.xmlui` to mirror.

input.content:
# Before

Bram's session/history surfaces are moving to Entire sessions +
checkpoints (see `entire-sessions-checkpoints-migration`). Entire only
captures work for agents that have been registered in the repo via
`entire agent add <name>` — `entire agent list` marks added agents with
a `✓`:

```
Agents:
  ✓ claude-code
    codex
    copilot-cli
    ...
```

Today nothing in Bram tells you when the agent you're driving is *not*
registered with Entire for this repo. If you switch Bram's agent to,
say, Codex (via the agent switcher) without having run
`entire agent add codex`, everything you do runs untracked: no
checkpoints, no session record, and it silently vanishes from the
Sessions tab (which, post-migration, is sourced from Entire). The gap
is invisible exactly when it matters.

Bram already knows the active agent — `enhanceStatus.activeProvider`
and the agent switcher state (`agentSwitcherSelected`,
`window.bramAgentMenu`, `__bramAgentProviderFromSettings` in
`helpers.js`) — but never cross-checks it against Entire's registered
set.

# After

Surface a dismissible banner in the agent pane when the **active agent
is not `✓`-registered in `entire agent list` for this repo**, prompting
the user to run `entire agent add <agent>` so their work is tracked.

- **Backend** — new route in `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (e.g.
  `/__entire/agent-status`) that shells out to `entire agent list`,
  parses the `✓`-marked lines into a registered-agent set, maps Bram's
  active provider (`claude` → `claude-code`, `codex` → `codex`) to the
  Entire agent id, and returns `{ activeAgent, registered: bool,
  addedAgents: [...] }`. Parsing the text is fine (no `--json` on
  `agent list`); the `✓ <id>` format is stable. Degrade quietly to
  "registered: true" on any `entire` failure so we never false-alarm.
- **UI** — a banner at the top of `app/tools/Main.xmlui`, mirroring the
  existing update-banner pattern (`updateBannerDismissedVersion` +
  `readLocalStorage`/dismiss). Show when `registered == false`; copy
  names the exact command (`entire agent add <agent>`); dismissible per
  agent so it reappears if you switch to a different un-added agent.

Alternatives considered:

- **Fire only at switch time (toast)** — rejected: a session already
  running under an un-added agent (e.g. Bram launched that way) would
  never see it. A standing banner keyed off the live active agent covers
  both switch and launch.
- **Block the switch until added** — rejected: too heavy-handed; the
  user may deliberately want an untracked run. Warn, don't gate.
- **[chosen] Standing, dismissible banner** driven by a backend
  agent-status route.

Scope notes:

- This is a **warning surface only** — it does not run `entire agent
  add` for the user (that mutates repo config; leave it explicit). Copy
  can offer the command to copy/run.
- Touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, shared with the paused
  `migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` and the
  `entire-sessions-checkpoints-migration` items. No on-disk conflict
  while those are unapplied, but whichever lands first, rebase the
  others' `lib.rs` edits.
- Natural companion to the sessions migration (it explains *why* a
  session might be missing), but independently shippable.

content.content:
60	    only advances at checkpoint boundaries.
61	  - **Historical / committed / teammate sessions** — Entire is the
62	    *better* source: durable (survives provider-file cleanup),
63	    commit-anchored ("transcript as of commit X"), and portable across a
64	    team via the checkpoints branch — none of which Bram's direct read
65	    can do.
66	
67	- **Search → genuinely additive.** `entire checkpoint search` (semantic +
68	  keyword across checkpoints/commits/sessions) is a capability Bram
69	  lacks today. Not a like-for-like swap of `/__sessions/search`'s
70	  transcript grep, but a strong new affordance.
71	
72	- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
73	  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Not session/checkpoint concepts.
74	
75	Remaining caveats (small): `--raw-transcript`/`--transcript` are
76	addressed per checkpoint ID / commit SHA; `--json` on a *temporary*
77	(uncommitted) checkpoint returned "not found", so committed checkpoints
78	are the reliable target. The transcript modes are "checkpoints v1"
79	("same bytes … while checkpoints v1 is the checkpoint store") — format
80	may shift.
81	
82	## Recommended scope, given the corrected finding
83	
84	**Union, not replacement — and it's now a bigger, better win than the
85	list-only slice.** The strongest design:
86	
87	- **Phase 1 (this item): session list = union.** Merge
88	  `entire session list --json` with the existing JSONL-derived list so
89	  the tab gains Entire's richer fields (tokens, checkpoints, branch)
90	  *and* keeps full coverage of untracked/historical/pre-`enable`
91	  sessions. Swap in `lib.rs` (`/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta`),
92	  rebind `Sessions.xmlui`.
93	- **Phase 2 (later item): transcript source = active-vs-historical
94	  split.** Live session keeps the direct provider read; historical /
95	  committed sessions can source `/__turns` from
96	  `entire checkpoint explain --raw-transcript`, gaining durable +
97	  commit-anchored + teammate-portable transcripts. Same projection
98	  code, new byte source.
99	- **Phase 3 (later item): add `entire checkpoint search`** as a *new*
100	  search affordance alongside (not replacing) `/__sessions/search`.
101	
102	Alternatives considered:
103	
104	- **Replace provider read entirely with Entire** — rejected: loses the
105	  live tail for the active session and coverage of untracked sessions.
106	- **Keep everything Bram-native (do nothing)** — rejected: forgoes
107	  durable/portable history and semantic search that Entire gives for
108	  free.
109	- **[chosen] Union for the list now; active-vs-historical transcript
110	  split and additive search as follow-ups.**
111	
112	Open decision before applying Phase 1 (needs the user):
113	
114	- Confirm **union** for the session list (recommended), vs. plain
115	  replace (simpler, but untracked/historical sessions vanish).
116	
117	Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which the paused
118	`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item also touches. No on-disk
119	conflict while that one is unapplied.

file.content:
only advances at checkpoint boundaries.
  - **Historical / committed / teammate sessions** — Entire is the
    *better* source: durable (survives provider-file cleanup),
    commit-anchored ("transcript as of commit X"), and portable across a
    team via the checkpoints branch — none of which Bram's direct read
    can do.

- **Search → genuinely additive.** `entire checkpoint search` (semantic +
  keyword across checkpoints/commits/sessions) is a capability Bram
  lacks today. Not a like-for-like swap of `/__sessions/search`'s
  transcript grep, but a strong new affordance.

- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Not session/checkpoint concepts.

Remaining caveats (small): `--raw-transcript`/`--transcript` are
addressed per checkpoint ID / commit SHA; `--json` on a *temporary*
(uncommitted) checkpoint returned "not found", so committed checkpoints
are the reliable target. The transcript modes are "checkpoints v1"
("same bytes … while checkpoints v1 is the checkpoint store") — format
may shift.

## Recommended scope, given the corrected finding

**Union, not replacement — and it's now a bigger, better win than the
list-only slice.** The strongest design:

- **Phase 1 (this item): session list = union.** Merge
  `entire session list --json` with the existing JSONL-derived list so
  the tab gains Entire's richer fields (tokens, checkpoints, branch)
  *and* keeps full coverage of untracked/historical/pre-`enable`
  sessions. Swap in `lib.rs` (`/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta`),
  rebind `Sessions.xmlui`.
- **Phase 2 (later item): transcript source = active-vs-historical
  split.** Live session keeps the direct provider read; historical /
  committed sessions can source `/__turns` from
  `entire checkpoint explain --raw-transcript`, gaining durable +
  commit-anchored + teammate-portable transcripts. Same projection
  code, new byte source.
- **Phase 3 (later item): add `entire checkpoint search`** as a *new*
  search affordance alongside (not replacing) `/__sessions/search`.

Alternatives considered:

- **Replace provider read entirely with Entire** — rejected: loses the
  live tail for the active session and coverage of untracked sessions.
- **Keep everything Bram-native (do nothing)** — rejected: forgoes
  durable/portable history and semantic search that Entire gives for
  free.
- **[chosen] Union for the list now; active-vs-historical transcript
  split and additive search as follow-ups.**

Open decision before applying Phase 1 (needs the user):

- Confirm **union** for the session list (recommended), vs. plain
  replace (simpler, but untracked/historical sessions vanish).

Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which the paused
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item also touches. No on-disk
conflict while that one is unapplied.

input.new_string:
## Agreed design for Phase 1 (checkpoint-based, plain replace)

Decisions locked with the user:

- **Plain replace, not union.** User just started with Bram and Entire
  was enabled from day one, so there are no untracked/pre-`enable`
  sessions to preserve. Accepts the tradeoffs surfaced by the
  falsification pass below.
- **Label = first prompt, prompt-as-label is fine** ("that's how the
  web UI works" — confirmed by a screenshot of Entire's own Sessions
  view labeling rows by their opening prompt).
- **Source = checkpoints, not `entire session list`.** The falsification
  pass showed `entire session list` is *global* (no repo scope flag) and
  only carries `last_prompt` (the latest prompt — for Bram sessions
  usually an internal transport frame like `Read and follow this Bram
  turn: @…`, not a title). Checkpoints solve both:
  - `entire checkpoint explain --json` (no target) returns a
    **repo+branch-scoped** array of `{checkpoint_id, session_id, date,
    message}`. Grouped by `session_id`, this reproduces the web UI:
    repo-scoped sessions, first-checkpoint `message` as the label, count
    per session, date grouping. No `worktree_path` filter needed —
    scoping is native.
  - Join `entire session list --json` on `session_id` only for the
    extras checkpoints don't carry: agent badge, model, tokens,
    active/ended status.
- **Branch-scoped with a selector.** Checkpoints are current-branch
  scoped (matches the web UI's branch dropdown). The tab becomes
  branch-scoped with a branch selector rather than spanning all
  branches.

Phase 1 build:

- **`lib.rs`** — reimplement `/__sessions/list` (+ `/__sessions/meta`)
  to shell `entire checkpoint explain --json` (repo+branch scoped),
  group by `session_id`, take earliest checkpoint `message` as the
  label + checkpoint count + date, and left-join `entire session list
  --json` on `session_id` for agent/model/tokens/status. Two `entire`
  calls, no provider-JSONL reading. Add a `branch` query param.
- **`Sessions.xmlui`** — rebind list rows to the new fields; add the
  branch selector.

Leave `/__turns`, `/__sessions/search`, `/rename`, `/delete` untouched
this phase (transcript-source split and semantic search are later
items).

Alternatives considered:

- **`entire session list` + `worktree_path` filter** — rejected: global
  list needs a bolted-on filter, and its `last_prompt` label shows Bram
  transport frames instead of the opening prompt.
- **Union (keep JSONL scan)** — rejected by the user: nothing untracked
  to preserve on this fresh setup.
- **[chosen] Checkpoint-scoped list + session-list join, branch
  selector, prompt-as-label.**

Accepted tradeoffs (from the falsification pass):

- No custom-title / Claude `aiTitle` labels — rows show the opening
  prompt (matches Entire's web UI).
- Sessions for agents not `entire agent add`-ed won't appear — covered
  by the companion `warn-active-agent-not-added-in-entire` item.
- Tab now depends on the `entire` binary + local checkpoint store being
  healthy (accepted).

Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, shared with the paused
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` and the
`warn-active-agent-not-added-in-entire` items. No on-disk conflict while
those are unapplied.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Before

Bram hand-rolls its own view of agent work across three tabs, each
backed by host routes in `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` that parse provider
session JSONL (Claude Code / Codex transcripts) directly:

- **Sessions tab** (`app/tools/components/Sessions.xmlui`) —
  `/__sessions/list`, `/__sessions/meta`, `/__sessions/search`,
  `/__sessions/rename`, `/__sessions/delete`, `/__turns`. Bram walks the
  transcript files itself to derive session lists, per-turn history, and
  token counts.
- **Context tab** (`app/tools/components/Context.xmlui`) —
  `/__context/list`, `/__context/file`, `/__context/search`. Views
  CLAUDE.md + memory + hooks + settings.
- **History tab** (`app/tools/components/History.xmlui`) —
  `/__worklist-history/list`, `/__worklist-history/search`. Bram's own
  worklist lifecycle audit.

`entire` already tracks this locally. `entire session list --json`
returns, per active/recent session: `session_id`, `agent`, `model`,
`status`, `branch`, `worktree_path`, `started_at`, `last_active`,
`turns`, `checkpoints`, `tokens{total,input,cache_read,cache_write,
output}`, `last_prompt`, `files_touched`. `entire session info
<id> --json` gives detail; `entire checkpoint list [--session <id>]`
lists checkpoints (agent-work records tied to commits); `entire
checkpoint explain` and `entire checkpoint search` (semantic + keyword)
cover per-checkpoint narrative and cross-session search.

# After

## Feasibility finding (2026-07-04, corrected)

**Feasible — and more so than a first pass suggested.** Correction to an
earlier wrong claim: `entire` *does* expose raw turn content. `entire
checkpoint explain <sha>` offers `--full` (parsed full transcript),
`--raw-transcript` and `--transcript` (raw JSONL). Verified:
`entire checkpoint explain 3c6b448 --raw-transcript` streams JSONL whose
lines are byte-shape-identical to the provider file Bram reads
(`~/.claude/projects/-Users-peterevjan-code-bram/<sid>.jsonl` — same
`leafUuid,sessionId,type` keys). Entire captures the *same* Claude
Code / Codex transcript, scoped to checkpoints (commit/turn boundaries),
and can stream it back.

So this is **not a concept mismatch — it's a *sourcing* choice.** Per
surface:

- **Session list metadata → clean swap.** `entire session list --json`
  carries everything Bram's list derives by hand plus more (`model`,
  `branch`, `status`, `turns`, `checkpoints`, full `tokens{...}`,
  `last_prompt`, `files_touched`). Local, real JSON today.

- **Transcript → same bytes, sourcing decision.** `/__turns`
  (`read_session_turns`, `lib.rs:14581`) is a projection over provider
  JSONL that also resolves Bram-only artifacts (outbound-turn envelopes,
  message/feedback draft refs, image markers, Codex fanout dedupe).
  Because `--raw-transcript` emits the *same JSONL*, that projection
  works unchanged on Entire's bytes. Tradeoffs:
  - **Live/active session** — keep reading the provider file directly:
    lower latency, live tail, no per-checkpoint shelling. Entire's copy
    only advances at checkpoint boundaries.
  - **Historical / committed / teammate sessions** — Entire is the
    *better* source: durable (survives provider-file cleanup),
    commit-anchored ("transcript as of commit X"), and portable across a
    team via the checkpoints branch — none of which Bram's direct read
    can do.

- **Search → genuinely additive.** `entire checkpoint search` (semantic +
  keyword across checkpoints/commits/sessions) is a capability Bram
  lacks today. Not a like-for-like swap of `/__sessions/search`'s
  transcript grep, but a strong new affordance.

- **History tab (worklist lifecycle) & Context tab (CLAUDE.md / memory /
  hooks / settings) → out of scope.** Not session/checkpoint concepts.

Remaining caveats (small): `--raw-transcript`/`--transcript` are
addressed per checkpoint ID / commit SHA; `--json` on a *temporary*
(uncommitted) checkpoint returned "not found", so committed checkpoints
are the reliable target. The transcript modes are "checkpoints v1"
("same bytes … while checkpoints v1 is the checkpoint store") — format
may shift.

## Recommended scope, given the corrected finding

**Union, not replacement — and it's now a bigger, better win than the
list-only slice.** The strongest design:

- **Phase 1 (this item): session list = union.** Merge
  `entire session list --json` with the existing JSONL-derived list so
  the tab gains Entire's richer fields (tokens, checkpoints, branch)
  *and* keeps full coverage of untracked/historical/pre-`enable`
  sessions. Swap in `lib.rs` (`/__sessions/list` + `/__sessions/meta`),
  rebind `Sessions.xmlui`.
- **Phase 2 (later item): transcript source = active-vs-historical
  split.** Live session keeps the direct provider read; historical /
  committed sessions can source `/__turns` from
  `entire checkpoint explain --raw-transcript`, gaining durable +
  commit-anchored + teammate-portable transcripts. Same projection
  code, new byte source.
- **Phase 3 (later item): add `entire checkpoint search`** as a *new*
  search affordance alongside (not replacing) `/__sessions/search`.

Alternatives considered:

- **Replace provider read entirely with Entire** — rejected: loses the
  live tail for the active session and coverage of untracked sessions.
- **Keep everything Bram-native (do nothing)** — rejected: forgoes
  durable/portable history and semantic search that Entire gives for
  free.
- **[chosen] Union for the list now; active-vs-historical transcript
  split and additive search as follow-ups.**

Open decision before applying Phase 1 (needs the user):

- Confirm **union** for the session list (recommended), vs. plain
  replace (simpler, but untracked/historical sessions vanish).

Sequencing note: touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which the paused
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item also touches. No on-disk
conflict while that one is unapplied.

aiTitle: Migrate from gh CLI to entire CLI with trails

content.text:
Both done:

- **`warn-active-agent-not-added-in-entire`** — new, in the Worklist tab (TO APPLY). Backend route parses `entire agent list`'s `✓` lines, maps Bram's active provider to the Entire agent id, and a dismissible banner in `Main.xmlui` fires when the active agent isn't registered. Warn-don't-gate; it doesn't run `entire agent add` for you.
- **`entire-sessions-checkpoints-migration`** — draft rewritten to the locked design: checkpoint-sourced (`entire checkpoint explain --json`, repo+branch scoped) grouped by `session_id`, first-prompt labels, joined with `entire session list` for agent/tokens/status, branch selector. The accepted tradeoffs are recorded in the draft.

On implementing: your "green light" told me to lock the design into the draft, which I've done — but the actual code apply runs through the **Approve** button, not a chat green light (that's the gate that authorizes me to edit `lib.rs`/`Sessions.xmlui`, and the design changed materially since your earlier Approve). So: click **Approve** on `entire-sessions-checkpoints-migration` in the Worklist tab and I'll implement Phase 1 and verify it against the live local checkpoint data. You can approve `warn-active-agent-not-added-in-entire` in the same pass or separately.

One heads-up on order: all three items touch `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`. They don't collide while unapplied, but once one lands, I'll rebase the others' `lib.rs` edits on it — so approving them one at a time (rather than all at once) will keep each commit's diff clean.

23	    id="sessionsMeta"
24	    url="/__sessions/meta"
25	    onLoaded="(data) => {
26	      if (!data || !data.provider) return;
27	      if (currentProvider !== data.provider) {
28	        currentProvider = data.provider;
29	        expandedId = null;
30	        expandedProvider = null;
31	        sessionsExpanded = false;
32	      }
33	    }" />
34	
35	  <DataSource
36	    id="sessionsFirstPage"
37	    url="/__sessions/list"
38	    queryParams="{{ limit: 20, provider: currentProvider }}"
39	    when="{!!currentProvider}" />
40	
41	  <DataSource
42	    id="sessionsFull"
43	    url="/__sessions/list"
44	    queryParams="{{ provider: currentProvider }}"
45	    when="{sessionsExpanded && !!currentProvider}" />
46	
47	  <!-- Projected turns for the expanded session from the single host
48	       projection (/__turns); this pane is a pure consumer — no client-side
49	       JSONL parsing (docs/turn-transport-redesign.md). -->
50	  <DataSource
51	    id="sessionContent"
52	    url="/__turns"
53	    queryParams="{{ id: expandedId, provider: expandedProvider }}"
54	    when="{!!expandedId && !!expandedProvider}" />
55	
56	  <DataSource
57	    id="searchResults"
58	    url="/__sessions/search"
59	    queryParams="{{ q: query, scope: scope, provider: currentProvider }}"
60	    when="{!!query && query.trim().length >= 2}" />
61	
62	  <variable
63	    name="sessionsSource"
64	    value="{(sessionsExpanded && sessionsFull.value) ? sessionsFull.value : (sessionsFirstPage.value || [])}" />
65	
66	  <variable
67	    name="displayedSessions"
68	    value="{window.selectDisplayed(query, searchResults.value, sessionsSource)}" />
69	
70	  <variable
71	    name="selectedSession"
72	    value="{!expandedId ? null : (displayedSessions.find(s => s.id === expandedId && s.provider === expandedProvider) || (sessionsSource || []).find(s => s.id === expandedId && s.provider === expandedProvider))}" />
73	
74	  <SearchHitModal
75	    id="searchHitModalRef"
76	    query="{query}" />
77	
78	  <ModalDialog
79	    when="{!!fullscreenImage}"
80	    fullScreen="true"
81	    onClose="fullscreenImage = null">
82	    <Image
83	      src="{window.imageSrcForPath(fullscreenImage)}"
84	      fit="contain"
85	      width="100%"
86	      height="100%" />
87	  </ModalDialog>
88	
89	  <APICall
90	    id="deleteSession"
91	    url="/__sessions/delete"
92	    method="get"
93	    queryParams="{{ id: $param.id, provider: $param.provider }}"
94	    confirmTitle="Delete session?"
95	    confirmMessage="This cannot be undone."
96	    completedNotificationMessage="Session deleted. Reload Bram to refresh the session list; you do not need to restart the agent."
97	    onSuccess="pendingDeletes = pendingDeletes.concat([lastDeleteId]); savePendingSessionDeletes(pendingDeletes); if (expandedId === lastDeleteId && expandedProvider === lastDeleteProvider) { expandedId = null; expandedProvider = null; } sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch();" />
98	
99	  <APICall
100	    id="renameSession"
101	    url="/__sessions/rename"
102	    method="get"
103	    queryParams="{{ id: $param.id, provider: $param.provider, title: $param.title }}"
104	    onSuccess="sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch();"
105	    onError="pendingRenames = pendingRenames.filter(id => id !== lastRenameId); savePendingSessionRenames(pendingRenames); renamingSession = { id: lastRenameId, provider: lastRenameProvider, title: lastRenameOriginalTitle }; renameDraft = lastRenameTitle" />
106	
107	  <ModalDialog
108	    id="renameDialog"
109	    when="{!!renamingSession}"
110	    onClose="renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''"
111	    title="Rename session">
112	    <VStack gap="$space-3" padding="$space-4">
113	      <TextBox
114	        id="renameBox"
115	        width="*"
116	        initialValue="{renamingSession && (renamingSession.title || '')}"
117	        autoComplete="off"
118	        autoCorrect="{false}"
119	        spellCheck="{false}"
120	        autoCapitalize="off"
121	        onDidChange="(v) => renameDraft = v" />
122	      <Text
123	        color="$textColor-secondary"
124	        fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
125	        value="Note: codex /resume can create additional bootstrap-only session files. Bram hides those from this list and keeps [current] on the live session file when it can identify it." />
126	      <HStack horizontalAlignment="end" gap="$space-2">
127	        <Button label="Cancel" variant="outlined" onClick="renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''" />
128	        <Button
129	          label="Rename"
130	          enabled="{renameDraft && renameDraft.trim().length > 0}"
131	          onClick="let target = renamingSession; let title = renameDraft; lastRenameId = target.id; lastRenameProvider = target.provider; lastRenameOriginalTitle = target.title || ''; lastRenameTitle = title; pendingRenames = pendingRenames.includes(target.id) ? pendingRenames : pendingRenames.concat([target.id]); savePendingSessionRenames(pendingRenames); renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''; toast('Session renamed. Reload Bram to see the new title in the session list.'); renameSession.execute({ id: target.id, provider: target.provider, title: title })" />
132	      </HStack>
133	    </VStack>
134	  </ModalDialog>
135	
136	  <PushSource
137	    id="sessionsListChangedEvt"
138	    initial="{null}"
139	    subscribe="{window.bramSubscribeTauriEvent('sessions-list-changed')}" />
140	  <ChangeListener
141	    listenTo="{sessionsListChangedEvt.value && sessionsListChangedEvt.value.tick}"
142	    onDidChange="() => { sessionsMeta.refetch(); sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch(); if (expandedId && expandedProvider) sessionContent.refetch(); }" />
143	  <HSplitter height="100%"
144	    initialPrimarySize="{window.__bramRestoreSplitterSize('sessions', '50%')}"
145	    onResize="(px) => window.__bramSaveSplitterSize('sessions', px)">
146	
147	    <VStack padding="$space-4" gap="$space-3" overflowY="auto">
148	      <HStack gap="$space-3" verticalAlignment="center">
149	        <Heading level="h2">Sessions</Heading>
150	        <SpaceFiller />
151	        <SearchBox
152	          placeholder="Search user/assistant text…"
153	          inProgress="{searchResults.inProgress}"
154	          onQueryChange="(q) => query = q"
155	          onClear="expandedId = null; expandedProvider = null; selectedSnippet = false; searchHitModalRef.close()" />
156	      </HStack>
157	      <Text
158	        when="{sessionsMeta.value}"
159	        fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
160	        color="$textColor-secondary"
161	        value="{sessionsMeta.value.count > 0 ? ('Auto-detected provider: ' + sessionsMeta.value.provider + ' (' + sessionsMeta.value.count + ' session' + (sessionsMeta.value.count === 1 ? '' : 's') + ')') : ('Auto-detected provider: ' + sessionsMeta.value.provider + ' (no sessions yet)')}" />
162	      <VStack
163	        when="{sessionsMeta.value && sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes && sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes.length > 0}"
164	        gap="$space-1"
165	        padding="$space-2"
166	        borderRadius="$borderRadius"
167	        backgroundColor="$color-warning-50">
168	        <Items data="{sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes}">
169	          <Text
170	            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
171	            color="$textColor-secondary"
172	            value="{$item}" />
173	        </Items>
174	      </VStack>
175	
176	      <VStack gap="0" width="100%">
177	        <HStack
178	          gap="$space-3"
179	          paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
180	          paddingVertical="$space-2"
181	          backgroundColor="$color-surface-100"
182	          borderBottom="1px solid $borderColor">
183	          <Text width="*" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="TITLE" />
184	          <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="140px" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="MODIFIED" />
185	          <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="70px" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="SIZE" />
186	        </HStack>
187	        <Items data="{displayedSessions}">
188	          <VStack gap="0" borderBottom="1px solid $borderColor">
189	            <HStack
190	              gap="$space-2"
191	              paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
192	              paddingVertical="$space-2"
193	              verticalAlignment="start"
194	              opacity="{(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id)) ? 0.5 : 1}">
195	              <Button
196	                label="⟳"
197	                variant="ghost"
198	                size="xs"
199	                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
200	                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Reload: kill the running agent and resume this session')}"
201	                onClick="window.__bramReloadAgentSessionClick($item.provider, $item.id, toast)" />
202	              <Button
203	                label="✕"
204	                variant="ghost"
205	                size="xs"
206	                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
207	                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Delete session')}"
208	                onClick="lastDeleteId = $item.id; lastDeleteProvider = $item.provider; deleteSession.execute({ id: $item.id, provider: $item.provider })" />
209	              <Button
210	                label="✎"
211	                variant="ghost"
212	                size="xs"
213	                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
214	                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Rename session')}"
215	                onClick="renamingSession = { id: $item.id, provider: $item.provider, title: ($item.title || '') }; renameDraft = ($item.title || '')" />
216	              <Link
217	                width="*"
218	                onClick="expandedId = $item.id; expandedProvider = $item.provider; selectedSnippet = false"
219	                noIndicator>
220	                <Text
221	                  width="100%"
222	                  fontWeight="{(expandedId === $item.id && expandedProvider === $item.provider) ? '$fontWeight-bold' : '$fontWeight-normal'}"
223	                  value="{($item.current ? '[current] ' : '') + ($item.title || '(untitled)')}" />
224	              </Link>
225	              <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="140px" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" value="{formatDateTime(new Date($item.mtime * 1000), 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm')}" />
226	              <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="70px" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" value="{Math.round($item.size / 1024) + ' KB'}" />
227	            </HStack>
228	            <Link
229	              when="{$item.snippets && $item.snippets.length > 0}"
230	              onClick="searchHitModalRef.open(($item.snippets || []).join(' … '), $item.title || '(untitled)')">
231	              <Text
232	                paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
233	                paddingBottom="$space-2"
234	                fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
235	                color="$textColor-secondary"
236	                value="…{$item.snippets.join(' … ')}…" />
237	            </Link>
238	          </VStack>
239	        </Items>
240	        <Link
241	          when="{(!query || query.trim().length < 2) && !sessionsExpanded && sessionsMeta.value && sessionsMeta.value.count > 20}"
242	          onClick="sessionsExpanded = true"
243	          noIndicator="true">
244	          <Text
245	            paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
246	            paddingVertical="$space-2"
247	            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
248	            color="$textColor-secondary"
249	            value="More ({sessionsMeta.value.count - 20} more)" />
250	        </Link>
251	        <Link
252	          when="{(!query || query.trim().length < 2) && sessionsExpanded && sessionsSource.length > 20}"
253	          onClick="sessionsExpanded = false"
254	          noIndicator="true">
255	          <Text
256	            paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
257	            paddingVertical="$space-2"
258	            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
259	            color="$textColor-secondary"
260	            value="Show fewer" />
261	        </Link>
262	      </VStack>
263	    </VStack>
264	
265	    <VStack
266	      when="{expandedId && expandedProvider}"
267	      padding="$space-4"
268	      gap="$space-3"
269	      overflowY="auto">
270	      <HStack gap="$space-2" verticalAlignment="center">
271	        <Text
272	          when="{selectedSession}"
273	          fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold"
274	          value="{(selectedSession.current ? '[current] ' : '') + (selectedSession.title || '(untitled)')}"
275	          width="*"
276	          maxLines="1" />
277	        <Button
278	          icon="down"
279	          variant="ghost"
280	          size="xs"
281	          tooltip="Scroll to bottom of this session"
282	          onClick="scrollBookmarkIntoView('end-of-expanded-session', true)" />
283	      </HStack>
284	      <Link
285	        when="{selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2}"
286	        onClick="selectedSnippet = false"
287	        noIndicator>
288	        <Text value="↥ Show full session" fontSize="$fontSize-xs" />
289	      </Link>
290	      <Text when="{sessionContent.inProgress && !sessionContent.loaded}">Loading…</Text>
291	      <Items
292	        when="{sessionContent.value}"
293	        data="{(selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2) ? window.__bramProjectedSessionTurns(sessionContent.value).filter(t => (t.text || '').toLowerCase().includes(query.trim().toLowerCase())) : window.__bramProjectedSessionTurns(sessionContent.value)}">
294	        <VStack
295	          gap="$space-1"
296	          paddingVertical="$space-2"
297	          borderTop="1px solid $borderColor">
298	          <Text
299	            fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
300	            fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold"
301	            color="{$item.role === 'user' ? '$color-info-700' : '$color-success-700'}"
302	            value="{$item.role}" />
303	          <Markdown content="{(selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2) ? paragraphsContaining($item.text, query) : $item.text}" />
304	          <VStack gap="$space-2" when="{$item.images && $item.images.length > 0}">
305	            <Items data="{$item.images}">
306	              <Link onClick="fullscreenImage = $item" noIndicator>
307	                <Image
308	                  src="{window.imageSrcForPath($item)}"
309	                  maxHeight="200px"
310	                  fit="contain" />
311	              </Link>
312	            </Items>
313	          </VStack>
314	        </VStack>
315	      </Items>
316	      <HStack width="100%" horizontalAlignment="end" bookmark="end-of-expanded-session" scrollMarginTop="$space-16">
317	        <Button
318	          icon="up"
319	          variant="ghost"
320	          size="xs"
321	          tooltip="Scroll to top"
322	          onClick="window.scrollAllToTop()" />
323	      </HStack>
324	    </VStack>
325	
326	  </HSplitter>
327	</Component>

<Component
  name="Sessions"
  var.currentProvider="{null}"
  var.expandedId="{null}"
  var.expandedProvider="{null}"
  var.fullscreenImage="{null}"
  var.query=""
  var.scope="all"
  var.sessionsExpanded="{false}"
  var.selectedSnippet="{false}"
  var.renamingSession="{null}"
  var.renameDraft=""
  var.pendingDeletes="{loadPendingSessionDeletes()}"
  var.pendingRenames="{loadPendingSessionRenames()}"
  var.lastDeleteId="{null}"
  var.lastDeleteProvider="{null}"
  var.lastRenameId="{null}"
  var.lastRenameProvider="{null}"
  var.lastRenameOriginalTitle=""
  var.lastRenameTitle="">

  <DataSource
    id="sessionsMeta"
    url="/__sessions/meta"
    onLoaded="(data) => {
      if (!data || !data.provider) return;
      if (currentProvider !== data.provider) {
        currentProvider = data.provider;
        expandedId = null;
        expandedProvider = null;
        sessionsExpanded = false;
      }
    }" />

  <DataSource
    id="sessionsFirstPage"
    url="/__sessions/list"
    queryParams="{{ limit: 20, provider: currentProvider }}"
    when="{!!currentProvider}" />

  <DataSource
    id="sessionsFull"
    url="/__sessions/list"
    queryParams="{{ provider: currentProvider }}"
    when="{sessionsExpanded && !!currentProvider}" />

  <!-- Projected turns for the expanded session from the single host
       projection (/__turns); this pane is a pure consumer — no client-side
       JSONL parsing (docs/turn-transport-redesign.md). -->
  <DataSource
    id="sessionContent"
    url="/__turns"
    queryParams="{{ id: expandedId, provider: expandedProvider }}"
    when="{!!expandedId && !!expandedProvider}" />

  <DataSource
    id="searchResults"
    url="/__sessions/search"
    queryParams="{{ q: query, scope: scope, provider: currentProvider }}"
    when="{!!query && query.trim().length >= 2}" />

  <variable
    name="sessionsSource"
    value="{(sessionsExpanded && sessionsFull.value) ? sessionsFull.value : (sessionsFirstPage.value || [])}" />

  <variable
    name="displayedSessions"
    value="{window.selectDisplayed(query, searchResults.value, sessionsSource)}" />

  <variable
    name="selectedSession"
    value="{!expandedId ? null : (displayedSessions.find(s => s.id === expandedId && s.provider === expandedProvider) || (sessionsSource || []).find(s => s.id === expandedId && s.provider === expandedProvider))}" />

  <SearchHitModal
    id="searchHitModalRef"
    query="{query}" />

  <ModalDialog
    when="{!!fullscreenImage}"
    fullScreen="true"
    onClose="fullscreenImage = null">
    <Image
      src="{window.imageSrcForPath(fullscreenImage)}"
      fit="contain"
      width="100%"
      height="100%" />
  </ModalDialog>

  <APICall
    id="deleteSession"
    url="/__sessions/delete"
    method="get"
    queryParams="{{ id: $param.id, provider: $param.provider }}"
    confirmTitle="Delete session?"
    confirmMessage="This cannot be undone."
    completedNotificationMessage="Session deleted. Reload Bram to refresh the session list; you do not need to restart the agent."
    onSuccess="pendingDeletes = pendingDeletes.concat([lastDeleteId]); savePendingSessionDeletes(pendingDeletes); if (expandedId === lastDeleteId && expandedProvider === lastDeleteProvider) { expandedId = null; expandedProvider = null; } sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch();" />

  <APICall
    id="renameSession"
    url="/__sessions/rename"
    method="get"
    queryParams="{{ id: $param.id, provider: $param.provider, title: $param.title }}"
    onSuccess="sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch();"
    onError="pendingRenames = pendingRenames.filter(id => id !== lastRenameId); savePendingSessionRenames(pendingRenames); renamingSession = { id: lastRenameId, provider: lastRenameProvider, title: lastRenameOriginalTitle }; renameDraft = lastRenameTitle" />

  <ModalDialog
    id="renameDialog"
    when="{!!renamingSession}"
    onClose="renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''"
    title="Rename session">
    <VStack gap="$space-3" padding="$space-4">
      <TextBox
        id="renameBox"
        width="*"
        initialValue="{renamingSession && (renamingSession.title || '')}"
        autoComplete="off"
        autoCorrect="{false}"
        spellCheck="{false}"
        autoCapitalize="off"
        onDidChange="(v) => renameDraft = v" />
      <Text
        color="$textColor-secondary"
        fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
        value="Note: codex /resume can create additional bootstrap-only session files. Bram hides those from this list and keeps [current] on the live session file when it can identify it." />
      <HStack horizontalAlignment="end" gap="$space-2">
        <Button label="Cancel" variant="outlined" onClick="renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''" />
        <Button
          label="Rename"
          enabled="{renameDraft && renameDraft.trim().length > 0}"
          onClick="let target = renamingSession; let title = renameDraft; lastRenameId = target.id; lastRenameProvider = target.provider; lastRenameOriginalTitle = target.title || ''; lastRenameTitle = title; pendingRenames = pendingRenames.includes(target.id) ? pendingRenames : pendingRenames.concat([target.id]); savePendingSessionRenames(pendingRenames); renamingSession = null; renameDraft = ''; toast('Session renamed. Reload Bram to see the new title in the session list.'); renameSession.execute({ id: target.id, provider: target.provider, title: title })" />
      </HStack>
    </VStack>
  </ModalDialog>

  <PushSource
    id="sessionsListChangedEvt"
    initial="{null}"
    subscribe="{window.bramSubscribeTauriEvent('sessions-list-changed')}" />
  <ChangeListener
    listenTo="{sessionsListChangedEvt.value && sessionsListChangedEvt.value.tick}"
    onDidChange="() => { sessionsMeta.refetch(); sessionsFirstPage.refetch(); if (sessionsExpanded) sessionsFull.refetch(); if (expandedId && expandedProvider) sessionContent.refetch(); }" />
  <HSplitter height="100%"
    initialPrimarySize="{window.__bramRestoreSplitterSize('sessions', '50%')}"
    onResize="(px) => window.__bramSaveSplitterSize('sessions', px)">

    <VStack padding="$space-4" gap="$space-3" overflowY="auto">
      <HStack gap="$space-3" verticalAlignment="center">
        <Heading level="h2">Sessions</Heading>
        <SpaceFiller />
        <SearchBox
          placeholder="Search user/assistant text…"
          inProgress="{searchResults.inProgress}"
          onQueryChange="(q) => query = q"
          onClear="expandedId = null; expandedProvider = null; selectedSnippet = false; searchHitModalRef.close()" />
      </HStack>
      <Text
        when="{sessionsMeta.value}"
        fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
        color="$textColor-secondary"
        value="{sessionsMeta.value.count > 0 ? ('Auto-detected provider: ' + sessionsMeta.value.provider + ' (' + sessionsMeta.value.count + ' session' + (sessionsMeta.value.count === 1 ? '' : 's') + ')') : ('Auto-detected provider: ' + sessionsMeta.value.provider + ' (no sessions yet)')}" />
      <VStack
        when="{sessionsMeta.value && sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes && sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes.length > 0}"
        gap="$space-1"
        padding="$space-2"
        borderRadius="$borderRadius"
        backgroundColor="$color-warning-50">
        <Items data="{sessionsMeta.value.discoveryNotes}">
          <Text
            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
            color="$textColor-secondary"
            value="{$item}" />
        </Items>
      </VStack>

      <VStack gap="0" width="100%">
        <HStack
          gap="$space-3"
          paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
          paddingVertical="$space-2"
          backgroundColor="$color-surface-100"
          borderBottom="1px solid $borderColor">
          <Text width="*" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="TITLE" />
          <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="140px" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="MODIFIED" />
          <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="70px" fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" color="$textColor-secondary" value="SIZE" />
        </HStack>
        <Items data="{displayedSessions}">
          <VStack gap="0" borderBottom="1px solid $borderColor">
            <HStack
              gap="$space-2"
              paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
              paddingVertical="$space-2"
              verticalAlignment="start"
              opacity="{(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id)) ? 0.5 : 1}">
              <Button
                label="⟳"
                variant="ghost"
                size="xs"
                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Reload: kill the running agent and resume this session')}"
                onClick="window.__bramReloadAgentSessionClick($item.provider, $item.id, toast)" />
              <Button
                label="✕"
                variant="ghost"
                size="xs"
                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Delete session')}"
                onClick="lastDeleteId = $item.id; lastDeleteProvider = $item.provider; deleteSession.execute({ id: $item.id, provider: $item.provider })" />
              <Button
                label="✎"
                variant="ghost"
                size="xs"
                enabled="{!(pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) || pendingRenames.includes($item.id))}"
                tooltip="{pendingDeletes.includes($item.id) ? 'delete pending' : (pendingRenames.includes($item.id) ? 'rename pending' : 'Rename session')}"
                onClick="renamingSession = { id: $item.id, provider: $item.provider, title: ($item.title || '') }; renameDraft = ($item.title || '')" />
              <Link
                width="*"
                onClick="expandedId = $item.id; expandedProvider = $item.provider; selectedSnippet = false"
                noIndicator>
                <Text
                  width="100%"
                  fontWeight="{(expandedId === $item.id && expandedProvider === $item.provider) ? '$fontWeight-bold' : '$fontWeight-normal'}"
                  value="{($item.current ? '[current] ' : '') + ($item.title || '(untitled)')}" />
              </Link>
              <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="140px" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" value="{formatDateTime(new Date($item.mtime * 1000), 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm')}" />
              <Text when="{!expandedId}" width="70px" fontSize="$fontSize-sm" value="{Math.round($item.size / 1024) + ' KB'}" />
            </HStack>
            <Link
              when="{$item.snippets && $item.snippets.length > 0}"
              onClick="searchHitModalRef.open(($item.snippets || []).join(' … '), $item.title || '(untitled)')">
              <Text
                paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
                paddingBottom="$space-2"
                fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
                color="$textColor-secondary"
                value="…{$item.snippets.join(' … ')}…" />
            </Link>
          </VStack>
        </Items>
        <Link
          when="{(!query || query.trim().length < 2) && !sessionsExpanded && sessionsMeta.value && sessionsMeta.value.count > 20}"
          onClick="sessionsExpanded = true"
          noIndicator="true">
          <Text
            paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
            paddingVertical="$space-2"
            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
            color="$textColor-secondary"
            value="More ({sessionsMeta.value.count - 20} more)" />
        </Link>
        <Link
          when="{(!query || query.trim().length < 2) && sessionsExpanded && sessionsSource.length > 20}"
          onClick="sessionsExpanded = false"
          noIndicator="true">
          <Text
            paddingHorizontal="$space-3"
            paddingVertical="$space-2"
            fontSize="$fontSize-xs"
            color="$textColor-secondary"
            value="Show fewer" />
        </Link>
      </VStack>
    </VStack>

    <VStack
      when="{expandedId && expandedProvider}"
      padding="$space-4"
      gap="$space-3"
      overflowY="auto">
      <HStack gap="$space-2" verticalAlignment="center">
        <Text
          when="{selectedSession}"
          fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold"
          value="{(selectedSession.current ? '[current] ' : '') + (selectedSession.title || '(untitled)')}"
          width="*"
          maxLines="1" />
        <Button
          icon="down"
          variant="ghost"
          size="xs"
          tooltip="Scroll to bottom of this session"
          onClick="scrollBookmarkIntoView('end-of-expanded-session', true)" />
      </HStack>
      <Link
        when="{selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2}"
        onClick="selectedSnippet = false"
        noIndicator>
        <Text value="↥ Show full session" fontSize="$fontSize-xs" />
      </Link>
      <Text when="{sessionContent.inProgress && !sessionContent.loaded}">Loading…</Text>
      <Items
        when="{sessionContent.value}"
        data="{(selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2) ? window.__bramProjectedSessionTurns(sessionContent.value).filter(t => (t.text || '').toLowerCase().includes(query.trim().toLowerCase())) : window.__bramProjectedSessionTurns(sessionContent.value)}">
        <VStack
          gap="$space-1"
          paddingVertical="$space-2"
          borderTop="1px solid $borderColor">
          <Text
            fontSize="$fontSize-sm"
            fontWeight="$fontWeight-bold"
            color="{$item.role === 'user' ? '$color-info-700' : '$color-success-700'}"
            value="{$item.role}" />
          <Markdown content="{(selectedSnippet && query && query.trim().length >= 2) ? paragraphsContaining($item.text, query) : $item.text}" />
          <VStack gap="$space-2" when="{$item.images && $item.images.length > 0}">
            <Items data="{$item.images}">
              <Link onClick="fullscreenImage = $item" noIndicator>
                <Image
                  src="{window.imageSrcForPath($item)}"
                  maxHeight="200px"
                  fit="contain" />
              </Link>
            </Items>
          </VStack>
        </VStack>
      </Items>
      <HStack width="100%" horizontalAlignment="end" bookmark="end-of-expanded-session" scrollMarginTop="$space-16">
        <Button
          icon="up"
          variant="ghost"
          size="xs"
          tooltip="Scroll to top"
          onClick="window.scrollAllToTop()" />
      </HStack>
    </VStack>

  </HSplitter>
</Component>

content.text:
Confirmed: `entire checkpoint explain --json` has **no branch flag** (`--branch main` errors; it's current-branch by default, with only a slow `--search-all` for all branches). So a working per-branch *selector* isn't buildable from the CLI. That's a forced, minor deviation from the approved design — I'm proceeding with a **current-branch v1** (matches Entire's own default view) and deferring the selector; I'll flag it clearly rather than fake it. Everything else in the design holds.

Implementing the backend now.

README.md:146:The first time you launch `claude` or `codex` in a repo, Bram checks what that provider needs and prompts once if anything is missing — no prompt on later launches. Accepting it installs the same two things for either provider: the worklist conventions the agent reads each session, and a `PreToolUse` guard hook that blocks file edits not covered by an approved worklist item. A provider-neutral authorization record plus a watcher-revert fallback back the hook up.
README.md:151:Once you launch an agent through Bram's terminal, the agent pane checks what that provider still needs for the current repo and prompts only when setup is missing.
README.md:175:PreToolUse hooks are the generic extension point — both Claude Code and codex expose them — so the two adapters share the same shape: each runs *before* the agent invokes a tool, receives a JSON payload describing the pending call on stdin, and can exit 0 to allow, return a deny decision to block (stderr/permissionDecisionReason goes back to the agent as a tool error), or fail to launch.
README.md:177:That means first-run setup is provider-aware in when it prompts but provider-symmetric in what it installs: launching either `claude` or `codex` and accepting the prompt sets up the shared core, the Codex-side `AGENTS.md` guidance block, the Codex `developer_instructions`, the worklist guard hooks, and the permission-menu surfacing hooks.
README.md:185:- **Codex: repo-local AGENTS.md plus native hook enforcement.** Setup writes a marked Bram block into repo-root `AGENTS.md`, installs top-level `developer_instructions` in `~/.codex/config.toml`, and registers the Codex worklist guard as a native `PreToolUse` hook. Codex launches also receive the same concise worklist guidance as a startup seed. The app reinforces that with the shared local authorization record in `resources/.worklist-authorization.json` and the watcher-revert fallback as defense in depth.
README.md:200:The provider hooks validate direct edits to `resources/worklist.json`. Proposal authoring and iterate-time prose refinement are allowed there; mechanical prune / status-advance operations are expected to go through `POST /__worklist/mutate` instead. Both providers now reject direct worklist edits that remove items or change their `status`, which keeps the shared backend endpoint as the canonical state machine for `advance` / `prune`. The watcher-based fallback (compare old/new worklist snapshots, consult `resources/.worklist-authorization.json`, restore prior contents if the prune wasn't authorized) remains as defense-in-depth — it fires later than a native hook, but it covers the case where a hook fails to launch (e.g., Python missing) or where a future provider integration lacks a comparable extension point.
README.md:202:The hook is a Python script and needs Python 3 to run. On macOS and Linux it's invoked directly via its shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env python3`), so `python3` must be on PATH — almost always the case. On Windows it's invoked via `py -3 <path>`; the `py` launcher ships with the python.org installer and resolves Python via the Windows registry, independent of PATH. If Python isn't installed at all, Claude Code shows "Failed with non-blocking status code" for every Write/Edit and the validator is silently inert — writes still proceed, but the worklist guard isn't actually checking them. Install Python 3 to enable enforcement.
README.md:212:Bram autostarts an agent in the terminal at launch. Configure it under
README.md:224:- `agent` — which provider to launch, `claude` or `codex` (defaults to `claude`).
README.md:225:- `args` — optional extra arguments appended to the launch command.

content.text:
Found the whole picture. Here's what's going on in plain terms:

- The Bram window you're using right now is `~/.local/bin/bram` — an **installed copy** from an earlier build. It does **not** have the change.
- The version *with* the change is the fresh binary I just built: `src-tauri/target/debug/bram`.
- This terminal (where I'm typing) lives *inside* the running Bram, so relaunching Bram will end this chat — that's normal and fine; the code's on disk and the item's saved as TO COMMIT.

The cleanest fix is to drop the fresh build into the same place your `bram` command points, then relaunch. Let me stage that safely for you (backup first, and I use `mv` so the currently-running app isn't disturbed):

input.command:
set -e
# Back up the current installed binary (running app keeps its open copy), then
# put the freshly-built one in its place so `bram` picks up the change.
mv ~/.local/bin/bram ~/.local/bin/bram.bak
cp /Users/peterevjan/code/bram/src-tauri/target/debug/bram ~/.local/bin/bram
chmod +x ~/.local/bin/bram
ls -la ~/.local/bin/bram ~/.local/bin/bram.bak
echo "OK: ~/.local/bin/bram is now the fresh build (backup at bram.bak)"
input.description: Install fresh build over ~/.local/bin/bram with backup
